candidate_check.description: The SUSE operating system must use the invoking user's password for privilege escalation when using "sudo".

check_content_excerpt:
Verify that the sudoers security policy is configured to use the invoking user's password for privilege escalation.

> sudo egrep -ir '(rootpw|targetpw|runaspw)' /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.d* | grep -v '#'

/etc/sudoers:Defaults !targetpw
/etc/sudoers:Defaults !rootpw
/etc/sudoers:Defaults !runaspw

If conflicting results are returned, this is a finding.
If "Defaults !targetpw" is not defined, this is a finding.
If "Defaults !rootpw" is not defined, this is a finding.
If "Defaults !runaspw" is not defined, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Define the following in the Defaults section of the /etc/sudoers file or a configuration file in the /etc/sudoers.d/ directory:

Defaults !targetpw
Defaults !rootpw
Defaults !runaspw
title: The SUSE operating system must use the invoking user's password for privilege escalation when using "sudo".
